rules: Template rows are readiness scaffolds, not real leads.
A real lead requires explicit_reply_ok consent, a traceable proof note, and safe non-emergency scope.
Raw email addresses or message bodies must not be stored in tracker CSV files.
Matched UTM can increment KPI; unmatched UTM remains qualitative manual lead only.
Owned assets, Luna packs, or offer experiments open only after repeated same-guardian demand.

steps.release: Contact / keepsake / Luna request text is structured and importable.
steps.stop: Do not store raw email content in CSV; copy only the structured request fields and proof note.

steps.release: The request has explicit_reply_ok consent, reply email verified outside CSV, and safe non-emergency scope.
steps.stop: Do not use emergency, diagnostic, therapy-replacement, or sensitive-detail requests for marketing decisions.

steps.command: python3 tools/promotion_lead_text_import.py add --input <REAL_STRUCTURED_REQUEST.txt> --proof-note "email thread checked YYYY-MM-DD"
steps.release: A real non-template lead row exists with traceable proof and no raw email stored.
steps.stop: Do not create fake leads, inferred consent, or placeholder request rows.

steps.release: Lead summary, evidence checklist, demand gate, handoff, and offer quickstart reflect the real request.
steps.stop: Do not make lead or offer decisions from stale generated files.

steps.release: Same guardian / intake has repeated demand and a matching ready route.
steps.stop: Do not build paid, Luna, or affiliate experiments from one request or empty data.

rows.ownedAssetCommand: python3 tools/promotion_lead_writeback.py add --source contact --guardian iris --intake-type owned_asset_request --consent-status explicit_reply_ok --proof-note "email thread iris-owned request checked 2026-06-15"
rows.lunaCommand: python3 tools/promotion_lead_writeback.py add --source luna_page --guardian iris --intake-type luna_scene_request --consent-status explicit_reply_ok --proof-note "email thread iris-luna request checked 2026-06-15"
